summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by affirming his belief in the Bible as God's transformative word for our lives. He introduced the story of Jacob from Genesis, describing his dysfunctional family and his deceptive actions to steal his brother Esau's birthright for a bowl of stew. Pastor Travis used this as a powerful analogy for how we are tempted to trade God's lasting blessings for momentary, worldly pleasures. He emphasized that the devil always has a 'bowl of stew' ready to tempt us into compromise. The core of the message focused on Genesis 32:24-29, where Jacob wrestles with God. From this encounter, Pastor Travis taught that when we truly get a hold of God, our lives are changed 'from now on.' He outlined three enormous blessings from this divine encounter: God gives us a new identity, calling us royalty instead of by our past; He gives us a new future filled with hope and purpose; and He blesses us in our brokenness, using our weaknesses to keep us humble, dependent on Him, and to create a testimony of His glory. The sermon concluded with a call to surrender to Jesus, to grab hold of Him and not let go, and to trust that God is close to the brokenhearted, ready to heal and restore.
mainPoints: Do not trade God's eternal blessings for momentary, worldly pleasures—the devil always has a 'bowl of stew' ready to tempt us.
When we encounter and hold onto God, He blesses us with a new identity (royalty), a new future (hope), and purpose in our brokenness.
Our brokenness and 'limps' are blessings that keep us humble, dependent on God, and become a powerful testimony of His glory.

versesMentioned.text: Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.
The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.

So Jacob was left alone, and a man wrestled with him till daybreak. When the man saw that he could not overpower him, he touched the socket of Jacob’s hip so that his hip was wrenched as he wrestled with the man. Then the man said, “Let me go, for it is daybreak.” But Jacob replied, “I will not let you go unless you bless me.” The man asked him, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered. Then the man said, “Your name will no longer be Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with God and with humans and have overcome.” Jacob said, “Please tell me your name.” But he replied, “Why do you ask my name?” Then he blessed him there.
But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s special possession, that you may declare the praises of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!
Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the wasteland.
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.

Three times I pleaded with the Lord to take it away from me. But he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Genesis 32:26 - "I will not let you go unless you bless me."
Monday.action: Identify one area where you need God's blessing and commit to praying about it with persistence today.
Monday.prayer: God, I hold onto You today. I will not let go until You bless this area of my life. I trust in Your timing and Your goodness.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 - "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come..."
Tuesday.action: Write down an old label from your past (e.g., 'deceiver,' 'addict,' 'failure') and next to it write who God says you are now (e.g., 'royalty,' 'forgiven,' 'new creation').
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, thank You for my new identity in Christ. Help me to see myself the way You see me and to walk in the freedom of being Your child.

Wednesday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:11 - "For I know the plans I have for you... plans to give you hope and a future."
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection, asking God to give you a specific hope or vision for your future. Write down what comes to mind.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I choose to forget the former things. My hope is in You. Show me the new thing You are doing in my life and give me courage to follow Your plan.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 - "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."
Thursday.action: Acknowledge a current 'limp' or weakness in your life. Instead of asking God to remove it, ask Him to show you how His power can be displayed through it.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, Your grace is enough for me. I surrender my weakness and brokenness to You. Use it to keep me humble, dependent on You, and to tell a story of Your glory.

Friday.scripture: 1 Peter 5:8 - "Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around..."
Friday.action: Identify one 'bowl of stew'—a tempting compromise—that you are facing. Make a practical plan to avoid it today (e.g., avoid a place, call an accountability partner).
Friday.prayer: God, make me alert today. Help me to recognize the enemy's schemes and to choose Your eternal blessings over momentary pleasure. Strengthen me by Your Spirit.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Peter 2:9 - "But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood..."
Saturday.action: Do one thing today that reflects your identity as God's royal child. This could be serving someone, speaking words of life, or refusing to engage in gossip.
Saturday.prayer: King of Kings, thank You for calling me into Your royal family. Help me to live in a way that honors my true identity and declares Your praises to those around me.
